bioprojects.title: Borrelia miyamotoi LB-2001 Genome sequencing

description.title: Sample from Borrelia miyamotoi LB-2001

attributes.name: note
attributes.value: This organism was isolated from an Ixodes scapularis tick into Mus musculus by G.A. Scoles et al. (Vector Borne Zoonotic Dis. 1: 21-34, 2001) at Yale University. This strain was transferred to the submitter's laboratory in 2003, and was kept frozen at -80 deg. until infections of the SCID mice were carried out.

URL -- http://www.jgi.doe.gov
JGI Project ID: 1013289
Source DNA and organism available from Tim Lilburn at the American Type Culture Collection (ATCC) (TLilburn@atcc.org)
Contacts: Nikos Kyrpides (nckyrpides@lbl.gov)
Tanja Woyke (microbe@cuba.jgi-psf.org)
Whole genome sequencing and draft assembly at JGI
Annotation by JGI
The JGI and collaborators endorse the principles for the distribution and use of large scale sequencing data adopted by the larger genome sequencing community and urge users of this data to follow them. It is our intention to publish the work of this project in a timely fashion and we welcome collaborative interaction on the project and analysis.
(http://www.genome.gov/page.cfm?pageID=10506376)
Full annotations are available from IMG

bioprojects.title: Borrelia hermsii strain HS1 genome sequencing and assembly

description.title: Borrelia hermsii strain HS1 isolate Browne Mountain
description.comment: A strain of Borrelia hermsii identified in Ornithodoros hermsi tick in 1968 and isolated in laboratory mice by Willy Burgdorfer of the Rocky Mountain Laboratory of the National Institutes of Health. It was subsequently isolated in culture in Kelly's Medium by Herbert Stoenner of the RML and thereaftercloned by limited dilution.
